question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a block number of `Long.MIN_VALUE` or a very large negative number to `getEvmVersionForBlock()`, causing `floorEntry()` to return `null` and falling back to the default `EVM_VERSION`, potentially bypassing version-specific transaction validation and allowing gossip of malformed transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a block number just below a known EVM version boundary (e.g., `44029065` instead of `44029066`) to `getEvmVersionForBlock()`, causing the executor to use an older EVM version that processes the transaction differently, leading to inconsistent gossip behavior across nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getEvmVersionForBlock()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `Long.MAX_VALUE` as the block number to `getEvmVersionForBlock()`, causing `floorEntry()` to return the latest EVM version entry and potentially enabling execution of transactions with opcodes not yet supported by the gossip layer, causing gossip failures?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getEvmVersions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hierarchical fallback logic in `getEvmVersions()` — which first checks `evmVersions`, then `network.evmVersions`, then `DEFAULT_EVM_VERSION_MAP` — to trigger a race condition where a concurrent request modifies the `evmVersions` map between the `CollectionUtils.isEmpty()` check and the `return evmVersions` call, causing inconsistent EVM version selection and gossip failures?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getEvmVersions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `evmVersions` is a mutable `TreeMap` (not thread-safe) to trigger a `ConcurrentModificationException` during `CollectionUtils.isEmpty()` evaluation by sending concurrent requests, causing the EVM executor to crash and preventing gossip of all in-flight transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of `transactionProperties` (via `@Getter(lazy = true)`) to trigger a race condition during first access where two concurrent requests both call `buildTransactionProperties()`, resulting in one request receiving a partially-constructed property map that causes the upstream library to drop or misroute its transaction before gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of `versionedConfiguration` (via `@Getter(lazy = true)`) to trigger a race condition where the `ConfigProviderImpl` is initialized with a stale or incomplete `transactionProperties` map, causing the upstream library to use incorrect configuration and preventing gossip of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject an arbitrarily large number of key-value pairs into `transactionProperties`, causing the `ConfigProviderImpl` initialization to consume excessive memory and triggering an OOM error that prevents gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a property key with a null value, causing a `NullPointerException` in `Collections.unmodifiableMap()` or downstream consumers that aborts transaction processing and prevents gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a property with an extremely long key or value string, causing the `ConfigProviderImpl` to throw a parsing exception that aborts EVM initialization and prevents gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `contracts.throttle.throttleByGas=true`, re-enabling gas throttling in the upstream library and causing the EVM executor to throttle and drop transactions before they are gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `contracts.throttle.throttleByOpsDuration=true`, re-enabling ops-duration throttling and causing the EVM executor to throttle and drop transactions before they are gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `executor.disableThrottles=false`, re-enabling all throttles in the upstream executor and causing it to drop or queue transactions before gossip, effectively preventing their propagation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `nodes.minNodeRewardBalance=0`, removing the deliberately-set `Long.MAX_VALUE` guard and causing the upstream library to actually pay rewards to system accounts during transaction processing, altering transaction outcomes and potentially preventing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `nodes.preserveMinNodeRewardBalance=false`, disabling the minimum balance preservation guard and causing the upstream library to enter an unexpected state during reward processing that aborts transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `hedera.shard` value of `-1` or an out-of-range integer, causing the upstream library to throw an arithmetic or validation exception during transaction routing that prevents gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `hedera.realm` value of `Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing integer overflow in the upstream library's realm-based transaction routing logic and preventing gossip of transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a malformed `ledger.id` hex string (e.g., non-hex characters), causing `Bytes.wrap()` or downstream hex parsing to throw an exception that aborts EVM initialization and prevents gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `contracts.chainId` value of `0` or a negative number, causing the upstream library's chain ID validation to reject all transactions as invalid and preventing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `contracts.chainId` matching a different network (e.g., Ethereum mainnet chain ID 1), causing the EVM executor to accept Ethereum-native transactions that are incompatible with Hedera gossip format and preventing their propagation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `contracts.evm.version` value of `v0.0` or an invalid semantic version string, causing the `ConfigProviderImpl` to fail to parse the version and throwing an exception that prevents EVM initialization and gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `fees.simpleFeesEnabled=true`, enabling simple fee enforcement in the upstream library and causing all transactions that do not meet the simple fee threshold to be rejected before gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `jumboTransactions.allowedHederaFunctionalities` value that includes `ScheduleCreate` or other transaction types not originally allowed, enabling jumbo-sized scheduled transactions that overwhelm the gossip layer and prevent other transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject a `jumboTransactions.allowedHederaFunctionalities` value that removes `EthereumTransaction`, causing all Ethereum-format transactions to be rejected as non-jumbo and preventing their gossip?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/EvmProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: buildTransactionProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `props.putAll(properties)` override to inject `contracts.systemContract.scheduleService.scheduleCall.enabled=true` combined with a crafted schedule call that creates a recursive or self-referencing schedule, causing an infinite loop in the upstream library that prevents gossip of all transactions?
